body:
Fixes #40

## Summary

- Replaced the broken `.github/workflows/publish.yml` workflow with `.github/workflows/release.yml`, matching the npm trusted publisher configuration shown in the issue.
- Added a CI test gate before release, npm trusted-publishing setup, release-needed detection, npm publish verification, published-package smoke testing, and GitHub release creation.
- Added README badges for npm, GitHub releases, and the `release.yml` workflow.
- Added release/CI helper scripts and a workflow contract test.
- Preserved issue evidence, failed CI logs, template comparison artifacts, and case-study analysis in `docs/case-studies/issue-40/`.
- Stabilized the CLI/test runtime by preferring Node before Bun and fixing help/version, `-j` parsing, Node 24 helper loading, and CI cleanup paths covered by tests.

## Root Cause

The failed run `28167007698` used the old `Publish to NPM` workflow and ran `bun publish -p --access public`. The issue screenshot shows npm trusted publishing configured for workflow filename `release.yml`, so the repository workflow did not match the trusted publisher entry point. The old workflow also skipped tests and did not create GitHub releases.

The PR investigation also found two CI-only follow-ups after the workflow replacement: Node 24 exposed a different `yargs/helpers` export shape through `use-m`, and `test-parallel.mjs` could leave nested `git` work active while cleaning up its temp directory. Both are documented in the case study.

Case study: https://github.com/link-foundation/gh-pull-all/blob/issue-40-5461509fea36/docs/case-studies/issue-40/analysis.md

Trusted publishing evidence:

![Trusted publishing settings](https://github.com/link-foundation/gh-pull-all/blob/issue-40-5461509fea36/docs/case-studies/issue-40/trusted-publishing-settings.png?raw=true)

## Verification

- `npm run check:syntax` (log: `docs/case-studies/issue-40/final4-check-syntax.log`)
- `npm run check:line-limits` (log: `docs/case-studies/issue-40/final4-check-line-limits.log`)
- `node tests/test-release-workflow.mjs` (log: `docs/case-studies/issue-40/final3-test-release-workflow.log`)
- `node test-parallel.mjs` from `tests/` (log: `docs/case-studies/issue-40/final4-test-parallel.log`)
- `npm run release:needed` (`should_release=true` for `gh-pull-all@1.4.2`)
- `npm pack --dry-run`
- `npm test` (`Passed: 30/30 tests`, log: `docs/case-studies/issue-40/final4-npm-test.log`)
- GitHub Actions `Checks and release` run `28185559146`: `Test` passed on head SHA `0b3a36d65065f789184e2e0312b8b65948a2dd27`; `Release` was skipped on the PR event as intended.

## Notes

The actual trusted npm publish can only be fully verified in GitHub Actions on the release path. The workflow now grants `id-token: write` only to the release job and uses npm CLI setup compatible with npm trusted publishing.
title: Fix CI release workflow for trusted publishing
